sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the bottom-right panel's center piece had no effect at all (no animation, no board change). This is different from clicking on the top-left center piece which triggered an animation.
So far:
- Clicking top-left center piece (row 12, col 14): triggered flashing animation on bottom-right panel corners
- Clicking bottom-right center piece (row 44, col 48): no effect
Let me try clicking on other center pieces and also on the colored cells (not center pieces) to understand the mechanics better.
Let me also try clicking on the charcoal frame corners themselves, since those are the ones that flashed.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The animation showed the corner decorations (nodes 33, 35, 62, 63) flashing between gray and white. These are the corner pieces of the charcoal frame. Maybe clicking on center pieces "selects" them and the flashing indicates which panel is the target.
Let me try a different approach - click on the colored cells (not center pieces) to see if they change color or do something else.
Let me try clicking on a red cell in the top-left panel.

sections.content:
World model update: Clicking the bottom-right center piece had no effect. Clicking the top-left center piece triggered corner flashing on the bottom-right frame. The game mechanic is not yet clear.
Plan: Try clicking on colored cells (non-center) and on the frame corners to understand interactions better.
